new_classes.label_en: PGI Still Wine in Containers of 2 Litres or Less with High Actual Alcoholic Strength (CN 22042279)
new_classes.label_de: Stillwein mit g.g.A. in Behältnissen bis 2 Liter mit hohem tatsächlichen Alkoholgehalt (KN 22042279)
new_classes.definition_en: Still wine of fresh grapes with a Protected Geographical Indication (PGI), presented in containers of 2 litres or less, with an actual alcoholic strength exceeding 15 % vol, classified under CN code 22042279 (Chapter 22). See additional note 6(a) to Chapter 22.

coverage_explanation: The legal text identifies CN 22042279 as a PGI still wine in containers ≤ 2 L. The code sits alongside 22042280 in the same PGI/small-container branch; the distinguishing criterion between 22042279 and 22042280 is actual alcoholic strength (inferred from the CN structure: 22042279 covers > 15 % vol and 22042280 covers ≤ 15 % vol, consistent with the pattern established for the PDO siblings 22042174/22042178). The PGI quality, container volume ≤ 2 L, and high alcoholic strength (> 15 % vol) are captured. The reference to additional note 6(a) is a cross-reference that cannot be fully axiomatised without the content of that note; this reduces coverage slightly. The sibling disjointness with 22042280 is not explicitly encoded as a complement restriction here, reducing coverage marginally further.
